title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured

input_row.title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured
input_row.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
input_row.rationale: Where etcd encryption is used, it is important to ensure that the appropriate set of encryption providers is used. Currently, the aescbc, kms, and secretbox are likely to be appropriate options. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Get the EncryptionConfig file set for --encryption-provider-config argument. Verify that aescbc, kms, or secretbox is set as the encryption provider for all the desired resources.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure a EncryptionConfig file. In this file, choose aescbc, kms, or secretbox as the encryption provider. Default Value: By default, no encryption provider is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/ 2. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/issues/92 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/#providers

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The configuration for encryption providers (aescbc, kms, secretbox) is specified in the EncryptionConfig file, which is referenced by the kube-apiserver via the --encryption-provider-config flag.
- The EncryptionConfig file is not stored in the Kubernetes API or as a Kubernetes resource; it is a file on the control plane node’s filesystem.
- The only way to verify the actual encryption providers in use is to access this file directly on the host or via the kube-apiserver process arguments, which are not exposed via the Kubernetes API or kubectl.
- The audit procedure requires running OS-level commands (ps -ef, file inspection) on the control plane node, which cannot be performed via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the control plane node where the kube-apiserver is running.
2. Run the following command to find the --encryption-provider-config flag and the path to the EncryptionConfig file:
   ```
   ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver
   ```
3. Note the file path provided to the --encryption-provider-config argument.
4. Open and review the EncryptionConfig file at the specified path.
5. Verify that the encryption providers configured for all desired resources are one or more of: aescbc, kms, or secretbox.
6. Confirm that less secure providers (such as identity or aesgcm) are not used as the primary provider for any resource.
7. Document the providers in use and ensure they meet organizational security requirements.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11.1 PDF
